methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Realta Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $36M+ from a founding team led by Kieran Furlong, Cary Forest, Jay Anderson, Ben Lindley, and Oliver Schmitz. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers, though comparables such as Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power do not provide a sufficiently dense peer set at the same stage and valuation tier for reliable median-based triangulation.

The stage-progression method is applicable and represents the primary valuation framework. Using total capital raised ($36M) as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $72M. The peer-median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow comparables; while 8 nuclear energy peers exist, the intersection of same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier criteria returned fewer than 3 datapoints, making sector-wide medians insufficiently reliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $61M (low) to $83M (high), centered on $72M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression midpoint. Primary uncertainties include the absence of dense same-stage nuclear peers, which limits the precision of cross-sectional validation, and the applicability of standard venture step-up multiples to capital-intensive, regulated-sector dynamics.
